017package org.apache.juneau.rest.server.management;
018
019import java.util.*;
020import java.util.logging.*;
021
022/**
023 * {@link LogBackend} implementation for {@link java.util.logging java.util.logging} (JUL).
024 *
025 * <p>
026 * This is the default backend driven by {@link LoggersManager} when no {@link LoggersSettings} declares another
027 * one, and it carries the original v1 {@code /loggers} behavior so the JUL path is unchanged: reads report each
028 * logger's own ({@link Logger#getLevel() configured}) level (empty string when inherited), the root logger is
029 * keyed {@code "ROOT"}, and sets are process-lifetime-only via {@link Logger#setLevel(Level)}.
030 *
031 * @since 10.0.0
032 */
033// Singleton is appropriate for a JUL logging backend — single shared instance per JVM.
034@SuppressWarnings("java:S6548")
035public class JulLogBackend implements LogBackend {
036
037   /** Process-wide shared instance (stateless). */
038   public static final JulLogBackend INSTANCE = new JulLogBackend();
039
040   private JulLogBackend() {}
041
042   @Override /* LogBackend */
043   public Map<String,String> getLevels() {
044      var out = new TreeMap<String,String>();
045      var lm = LogManager.getLogManager();
046      var names = Collections.list(lm.getLoggerNames());
047      for (var name : names) {
048         var logger = lm.getLogger(name);
049         if (logger == null)
050            continue;
051         var level = logger.getLevel();
052         var key = name.isEmpty() ? "ROOT" : name;
053         out.put(key, level == null ? "" : level.getName());
054      }
055      return out;
056   }
057
058   @Override /* LogBackend */
059   public String getLevel(String name) {
060      var logger = LogManager.getLogManager().getLogger(resolveName(name));
061      if (logger == null)
062         return null;
063      var level = logger.getLevel();
064      return level == null ? "" : level.getName();
065   }
066
067   @Override /* LogBackend */
068   public void setLevel(String name, String level) {
069      var logger = Logger.getLogger(resolveName(name));
070      logger.setLevel(level == null || level.isBlank() ? null : Level.parse(level.trim()));
071   }
072
073   private static String resolveName(String name) {
074      // The root logger is the empty-string-named logger; expose it under the friendlier "ROOT" alias.
075      return (name == null || name.equals("ROOT")) ? "" : name;
076   }
077}
